docs.text: I am pursuing my PhD as a member of the STARS lab under the guidance of Prof. Sashikumaar Ganesan at the department of Computational and Data Sciences (CDS) in the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bengaluru, India. Experience ¶ May 2021 - April 2022 Exploring an evolution model and examining robustness of self organised criticality in the presence of random links; analysis of an interesting emergent “activity network” that we found in the system, under the guidance of Prof. Sudeshna Sinha; published in EPJP June - July 2021 Conducted help sessions for second year undergraduate course on Electrodynamics. May-July 2018 Computational work on one dimensional maps, understanding dimensionality of fractals, learnt and implemented the ‘small-world’ network, studied spatio-temporal characteristics of coupled lattice maps, under the guidance of Prof. Sudeshna Sinha Education ¶ BS-MS with a Physics Major at Indian Institute of Science Education and Research, Mohali with a CPI of 8.9 out of 10. Class 12 Complete in Kendriya Vidyalaya, IIT Kanpur with average of 93%. Skills ¶ Programming Languages C, C++, Python, MATLAB Software LATEX, shell scripting with basic UNIX programs including AWK scripting
